speaker: LORENZO
text: Nay, we will slink away in supper time,

speaker: LORENZO
text: Disguise us at my lodging, and return

speaker: LORENZO
text: All in an hour.

speaker: GRATIANO
text: We have not made good preparation.

speaker: SALARINO
text: We have not spoke us yet of torchbearers.

speaker: SOLANIO
text: ’Tis vile, unless it may be quaintly ordered,

speaker: SOLANIO
text: And better in my mind not undertook.

speaker: LORENZO
text: ’Tis now but four o’clock. We have two hours

speaker: LORENZO
text: To furnish us.

speaker: LORENZO
text: Friend Lancelet, what’s the news?

speaker: LANCELET
text: An it shall please you to break up this, it

speaker: LANCELET
text: shall seem to signify.

speaker: LORENZO
text: I know the hand; in faith, ’tis a fair hand,

speaker: LORENZO
text: And whiter than the paper it writ on

speaker: LORENZO
text: Is the fair hand that writ.

speaker: GRATIANO
text: Love news, in faith!

speaker: LANCELET
text: By your leave, sir.

speaker: LORENZO
text: Whither goest thou?

speaker: LANCELET
text: Marry, sir, to bid my old master the Jew to

speaker: LANCELET
text: sup tonight with my new master the Christian.

speaker: LORENZO
text:
Hold here, take this.
                 
                Giving
                 
                him
                 
                money
                .
               Tell gentle Jessica

speaker: LORENZO
text: I will not fail her. Speak it privately.

speaker: LORENZO
text: Go, gentlemen,

speaker: LORENZO
text: Will you prepare you for this masque tonight?

speaker: LORENZO
text: I am provided of a torchbearer.

speaker: SALARINO
text: Ay, marry, I’ll be gone about it straight.

speaker: SOLANIO
text: And so will I.

speaker: LORENZO
text: Meet me and Gratiano

speaker: LORENZO
text: At Gratiano’s lodging some hour hence.

speaker: SALARINO
text: ’Tis good we do so.

speaker: GRATIANO
text: Was not that letter from fair Jessica?

speaker: LORENZO
text: I must needs tell thee all. She hath directed

speaker: LORENZO
text: How I shall take her from her father’s house,

speaker: LORENZO
text: What gold and jewels she is furnished with,

speaker: LORENZO
text: What page’s suit she hath in readiness.

speaker: LORENZO
text: If e’er the Jew her father come to heaven,

speaker: LORENZO
text: It will be for his gentle daughter’s sake;

speaker: LORENZO
text: And never dare misfortune cross her foot

speaker: LORENZO
text: Unless she do it under this excuse,

speaker: LORENZO
text: That she is issue to a faithless Jew.

speaker: LORENZO
text: Come, go with me. Peruse this as thou goest;

speaker: LORENZO
text: Fair Jessica shall be my torchbearer.
